title: Rich Earthy Chinese Sweet Potatoes Shui Zhu Yu
description: A one-pot Chinese dish featuring sweet potatoes in a nimono-simmer style, resulting in an earthy and silky texture, suitable for a Jain-friendly diet. This dish is a variation of the traditional Shui Zhu Yu, adapted to incorporate sweet potatoes.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the sliced ginger and cook for 2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the cubed sweet potatoes and cook for 5 minutes, until they start to brown.
Add the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, and water, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, until the sweet potatoes are tender.
Stir in the chopped scallions and sesame oil.
Serve hot, garnished with additional scallions if desired.
